title: SVG→PDF pipeline + A4/Letter paper sizing
context: SVG is the source-of-truth output of the renderer; printable PDFs are the downstream artifact beneficiaries actually use. The SVG→PDF conversion must be deterministic, print-faithful (dimensions accurate to the target paper size), and based on an openly-licensed library. Both A4 and US Letter must be supported from the start because the project serves global learners. The chosen toolchain decision must be recorded and the library pinned.
objective: Select, integrate, and document an openly-licensed SVG→PDF conversion library; implement paper-size handling (A4 and US Letter, portrait, with correct margins); ensure the pipeline is deterministic and produces grayscale-safe, print-faithful PDFs.
acceptanceCriteria: SVG→PDF conversion library is openly licensed (MIT, Apache 2.0, or equivalent permissive license) and the choice is recorded with rationale in the repo.
Pipeline produces print-faithful PDFs in A4 (210×297 mm) and US Letter (8.5×11 in) at correct physical dimensions.
Output is deterministic: identical SVG + paper size → byte-stable PDF (or as close as the library allows, with any non-determinism documented).
PDFs are grayscale-safe by default (no color-only information critical to the sheet).
Pipeline integrated into packages/cli or packages/core and exercised by at least one end-to-end test.
Library version pinned; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md section 6 (Key decisions — SVG→PDF toolchain), section 16 open Q1
PLAN.md section 5 (Scope — A4 + US Letter, portrait, grayscale-safe)
renderer-005 (SVG output to be consumed by this pipeline)
output: SVG→PDF conversion integrated into the project build, with paper-size handling, a recorded toolchain decision, and end-to-end tests — committed via PR with DCO sign-off.
